frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tools are set up for task initiation.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is being loaded onto the driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle is held correctly, ensuring proper grip.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is placed through the Penrose drain.
First throw of the surgeon's knot in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Throw is performed with a double loop using the right hand.
Hand switch observed, maintaining appropriate technique.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw visible with hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch observed again, completing the knot.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting of suture ends is visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both ends cut close to the knot.

frame_analyses.description: Task appears completed with a secure knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final inspection of suture and knot security.

knot_assessments.notes: Secure finishing throw with hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit is closed with no visible gap.

technique_summary: The participant effectively completed the task with a secure and appropriate intracorporeal knot while maintaining hand switching. Minor deviation in suture placement occurred.
improvement_suggestions: Work on decreasing time during needle loading phase.
